source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A vertical stained glass square window of area $81 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ is made out of six triangles of equal area (see figure). A fly is sitting on the exact spot where the six triangles meet. How far from the bottom of the window is the fly sitting? <image1>

normalized_value.title: Dataset Open Question
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text: A vertical stained glass square window of area $81 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ is made out of six triangles of equal area (see figure). A fly is sitting on the exact spot where the six triangles meet. How far from the bottom of the window is the fly sitting?

agent_assessment.rationale: The text clearly asks for the fly's height above the bottom of a square window, and the answer format is a single distance. The diagram is required to understand the six equal-area triangles meeting at one interior point, and the image clearly shows that geometry well enough for a human annotator to follow the problem.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The sample from MathVision is semantically understandable and ready for annotation.
